request.question:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific.

In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

references.relevance_note: Involves doxorubicin-loaded PLGA nanoparticles, providing context on drug delivery mechanisms that may relate to the proposed study.

references.title: Combinatorial delivery of superparamagnetic iron oxide nanoparticles (γFe2O3) and doxorubicin using folate conjugated redox sensitive multiblock polymeric nanocarriers for enhancing the chemotherapeutic efficacy in cancer cells.
references.relevance_note: Explores folate conjugates in drug delivery, relevant to the functionalization aspect of the hypothesis.

literature_qc.explanation: The papers retrieved mention nanoparticles for drug delivery, including PLGA nanoparticles and doxorubicin, but none specifically address the combination of folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles and HeLa cells against free doxorubicin. For example, paper [4] discusses folate conjugated nanoparticles but does not explicitly involve a comparative study with doxorubicin on HeLa cells. Therefore, while related work exists, it does not present the exact match sought in the hypothesis.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To compare intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin delivered via folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles versus free doxorubicin in HeLa cells.
plan.experimental_design: This study involves the preparation of folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles loaded with doxorubicin. HeLa cells will be treated with both the nanoparticles and free doxorubicin. Intracellular drug accumulation will be assessed using fluorescence microscopy and quantified with flow cytometry, comparing both treatment groups directly to evaluate the efficacy of folate targeting.
controls: Free doxorubicin at 10 µM concentration in RPMI-1640 medium for 24 h treatment.
Untreated HeLa cells with no drug exposure in RPMI-1640 medium for 24 h.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Preparation
Dissolve 100 mg of PLGA (lactide:glycolide ratio 50:50, Resomer RG 503 H, Evonik Industries) in 10 mL of dichloromethane at room temperature.
Expected outcome: PLGA dissolves completely in dichloromethane.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Nanoparticle Preparation
Add 5 mL of an aqueous solution of folate (1 mg/mL, Sigma Aldrich) dropwise to the PLGA solution while stirring continuously.
Expected outcome: Homogeneous solution is obtained.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Nanoparticle Preparation
Evaporate the solvent using a rotary evaporator at 50°C until a viscous emulsion forms, approximately 1 h.
Expected outcome: Viscous emulsion forms with minimal residual solvent.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Nanoparticle Preparation
Add 10 mL of phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to the emulsion and vortex at high speed for 10 min.
Expected outcome: Emulsion transforms into stable nanoparticle dispersion.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Doxorubicin Loading
Dissolve 5 mg of doxorubicin (powder, DB2390, Selleck) in 1 mL of dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and add to the nanoparticle dispersion.
Expected outcome: Doxorubicin is fully dissolved and mixed with nanoparticle dispersion.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Doxorubicin Loading
Stir the mixture at room temperature for 2 h to allow for drug encapsulation.
Expected outcome: Physical entrapment of doxorubicin within nanoparticles.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Cell Treatment
Seed 1 × 10^5 HeLa cells in 1 mL of RPMI-1640 medium in a 24-well plate and incubate at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 h prior to treatment.
Expected outcome: HeLa cells adhere and reach approximately 70% confluency.

DAY 1 | 4 h | Cell Treatment
Treat cells with either 10 µM folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles loaded with doxorubicin or 10 µM free doxorubicin in 1 mL of RPMI-1640 medium for 4 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Cells treated with either formulation are subjected to drug uptake.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Measurement
Wash HeLa cells with PBS, then fix with 4% paraformaldehyde for 15 min at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Cells are fixed and prepared for subsequent analysis.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Measurement
Measure intracellular fluorescence using flow cytometry (BD LSRFortessa) with an excitation wavelength of 480 nm and emission collection at 575 nm.
Expected outcome: Fluorescence data collected, presenting drug accumulation levels.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Data Analysis
Compare mean fluorescence intensity from flow cytometry data for both treatment groups using unpaired t-test with significance set at p<0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined, confirming 3-fold greater accumulation in folate-functionalized PLGA groups compared to free doxorubicin.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed in HeLa cells facilitating endocytosis of the nanoparticles.
The drug delivery mechanism is similar in both formulations for comparative assessment.
Doxorubicin retains its bioactivity after encapsulation in PLGA nanoparticles.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin in nanoparticle-treated cells is at least 3-fold greater than in free doxorubicin cells.
Results are validated through replicates showing consistent data trends.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Standardize PLGA lot numbers for consistent nanoparticle characteristics.
Use the same HeLa cell stock for all experimental replicates to ensure uniformity.
Regularly calibrate flow cytometer to maintain accuracy in fluorescence measurements.

staffing.cro_delegation_recommendation: Consider CRO delegation for 'Day 0: Nanoparticle Preparation' due to the need for specialized equipment (rotary evaporator) and expertise in nanoparticle formulation, which may not be readily available in-house.
staffing.staffing_notes: The experiment involves multiple phases that require parallel execution, particularly during the cell treatment and measurement phases. The Research Associate will need to be trained in biosafety and flow cytometry operation. The bioinformatician will handle data analysis, which is critical for interpreting results.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To compare intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin delivered via folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles versus free doxorubicin in HeLa cells.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
